message.content: I can no longer paste any text into claude code over SSH.  If I exit claude code I can paste it to the terminal no problem.

addedBlocks:
## context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

attachment.content:
- audit: Verify a spec unit's implementation against its authoritative spec. Reads the manifest, reads spec files, walks target code, catalogs every gap, and writes task files for each finding.
- spec: Read the implementation spec for a given I/O module. Usage: /spec <module>. Modules: console, process, designer, graphics, display-elements, shapes, opc
- design-qa: Inventory all implementations of a cross-cutting feature across the codebase, surface inconsistencies, ask targeted design questions, and write the answers as an authoritative decision file. Run this BEFORE /audit on any feature marked ⚠️ NOT SPECCED in the manifest.
- spec-index: Print a quick-reference index of every audit unit — unit ID, canonical name, wave, and current task status. Use this to look up the correct unit ID or module name before running spec-scout or feature-agent.
- agent-guide: Print a reference guide for all custom agents in this project — what each one does, when to use it, how to launch it, and usage examples.
- wrapup: Close the current work unit (summary + review + doc updates)
- review: Run a verification review of work done since the last [initprompt]
- deepreview: Run a deep verification review including full file contents
- summarize: Generate an LLM-based summary of the current work unit
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

aiTitle: Debug SSH paste functionality in Claude Code

content.text:
This is a known friction point with Claude Code's TUI over SSH — it takes over the terminal and bracketed paste mode often breaks.

**Workarounds:**

1. **Write to a temp file first, then reference it.** In your SSH terminal (outside Claude Code), write what you want to paste to a file, then in Claude Code ask me to read it:
   ```
   # Outside Claude Code:
   cat > /tmp/paste.txt   # paste your content, then Ctrl+D
   ```
   Then in Claude Code: "read /tmp/paste.txt"

2. **Use the `!` prefix inside Claude Code.** Type `! cat > /tmp/paste.txt`, hit enter — this drops to a shell where paste works — paste your content, hit Ctrl+D. Now `/tmp/paste.txt` has your content.

3. **SSH with a different `TERM`.** Some terminals work better:
   ```sh
   TERM=xterm ssh user@host
   ```

4. **tmux/screen workaround.** If you're in tmux, `Ctrl+b ]` (tmux paste) or `set -g terminal-overrides` can help with bracketed paste conflicts.

**Root cause:** Claude Code enables bracketed paste mode (`ESC[?2004h`) for its input handling. Some SSH client/terminal combos don't forward the bracketed paste sequences correctly, so the paste content gets dropped or garbled.

If the temp file approach is the fastest fix for now — report the underlying bug at https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues so the team can track it.

message.content: It worked for months before this.

content.text:
Fair point — if it worked for months, something changed. Most likely a Claude Code auto-update.

Check your current version:
```
claude --version
```

And whether an update recently happened:
```
claude update --check
```

Also worth checking: did anything update on the SSH *client* side (terminal emulator, OS update, SSH client version)?

If it's a Claude Code regression, the fastest path is filing a bug at https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues — include your version, OS, terminal emulator, and SSH client. That's the right place for this; I can't fix the Claude Code binary itself.

message.content: Weird, I can't select out of this window either.  It like sends keystrokes?

content.text:
That's mouse reporting mode — Claude Code's TUI is capturing mouse events so your terminal can't use them for selection. Normally you can bypass it by holding **Shift** while clicking/dragging to select text.

The fact that both paste *and* mouse selection broke together points strongly at a recent Claude Code update changing how it initializes terminal modes. Before it apparently wasn't capturing the mouse; now it is.

**Immediate workaround:** `Shift+drag` to select text in your terminal.

**For the bug report:** https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues — mention both symptoms (paste broken, mouse selection captured) and your version from `claude --version`. That combination is a clear signal of a terminal mode regression.

lastPrompt: Weird, I can't select out of this window either.  It like sends keystrokes?

aiTitle: Debug SSH paste functionality in Claude Code

message.content: Shift drag copies and shift right click pastes is there some way to change this functionality back to the default?

content.text: Let me check if Claude Code has a setting to disable mouse capture.

input.prompt: Does Claude Code (the CLI tool) have any setting, flag, or configuration option to disable mouse reporting / mouse capture mode in the terminal? The user is over SSH and the TUI is capturing mouse events, preventing normal terminal text selection without Shift. Is there a --no-mouse flag, a settings.json option, or any other way to turn off mouse mode? Report in under 100 words.

content.text:
**No.** Claude Code does not have a mouse disable flag, environment variable, or settings option. The documentation covers terminal configuration, terminal setup, and fullscreen rendering, but does not mention mouse capture control.

**Workaround:** Over SSH, hold **Shift** while selecting text to bypass mouse mode capture and use native terminal selection. Alternatively, use fullscreen rendering mode (`/tui fullscreen`) which may reduce mouse interference, though this won't disable it entirely.

toolUseResult.prompt: Does Claude Code (the CLI tool) have any setting, flag, or configuration option to disable mouse reporting / mouse capture mode in the terminal? The user is over SSH and the TUI is capturing mouse events, preventing normal terminal text selection without Shift. Is there a --no-mouse flag, a settings.json option, or any other way to turn off mouse mode? Report in under 100 words.

content.text:
**No.** Claude Code does not have a mouse disable flag, environment variable, or settings option. The documentation covers terminal configuration, terminal setup, and fullscreen rendering, but does not mention mouse capture control.

**Workaround:** Over SSH, hold **Shift** while selecting text to bypass mouse mode capture and use native terminal selection. Alternatively, use fullscreen rendering mode (`/tui fullscreen`) which may reduce mouse interference, though this won't disable it entirely.

No setting exists for it. It's not configurable.

Your options:
- **Shift+drag / Shift+right-click** — what you're already doing, that's the intended bypass
- **File a bug** at https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues — if this regressed in a recent update, the team needs to know. Mention the version (`claude --version`) and that mouse capture + paste both broke at the same time over SSH

The paste issue is the more painful one — Shift+right-click pastes *to the terminal* but does it actually insert text into Claude Code's input, or does it paste somewhere else?

content.text:
No public changelog exposed via npm. You're on 2.1.150 and there have been ~20 releases in recent history, so something almost certainly changed in the TUI layer without being documented publicly.

The honest answer: I can't tell you exactly which version introduced this without digging through their git history, which isn't public. The rapid release cadence (they're shipping nearly daily) means a terminal handling change could have slipped in quietly.

If you want to know for sure, filing a GitHub issue and asking "which version changed mouse capture behavior over SSH" is the fastest way to get an answer from someone who can actually check the commit history.

content.content:
TERM=xterm
---
---
#!/usr/bin/env bash
input=$(cat)

model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.display_name // "Unknown"')
context_size=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.context_window_size // 200000')
used_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.used_percentage // empty')
total_in=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.total_input_tokens // 0')
total_out=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.total_output_tokens // 0')
cache_write=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.current_usage.cache_creation_input_tokens // 0')
cache_read=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.current_usage.cache_read_input_tokens // 0')
transcript=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.transcript_path // empty')

claude_md_lines=""
_claude_md_path=""
if [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ] && [ -f "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/CLAUDE.md" ]; then
    _claude_md_path="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/CLAUDE.md"
elif [ -f "${PWD}/CLAUDE.md" ]; then
    _claude_md_path="${PWD}/CLAUDE.md"
fi
if [ -n "$_claude_md_path" ]; then
    claude_md_lines=$(wc -l < "$_claude_md_path" 2>/dev/null)
fi

BASE_RATIO=0.30
CONV_RATIO=0.70
base_pct=$(echo "$used_pct $BASE_RATIO" | awk '{if($1=="")print 0; else printf "%.1f", $1*$2}')
conv_pct=$(echo "$used_pct $CONV_RATIO" | awk '{if($1=="")print 0; else printf "%.1f", $1*$2}')

BAR_WIDTH=20
if [ -n "$used_pct" ]; then
    ctx_display=$(echo "$base_pct $conv_pct $used_pct $BAR_WIDTH $context_size" | awk '
    BEGIN { OFMT = "%.0f" }
    {
        base_pct=$1; conv_pct=$2; used_pct=$3; W=$4; ctx_size=$5
        base_chars = int(base_pct/100*W+0.5)
        conv_chars = int(conv_pct/100*W+0.5)
        if (base_chars > W) base_chars = W
        if (base_chars+conv_chars > W) conv_chars = W-base_chars
        unused_chars = W-base_chars-conv_chars
        esc=sprintf("%c",27)
        yellow=esc"[33m"; cyan=esc"[36m"; gray=esc"[90m"
        bold=esc"[1m"; reset=esc"[0m"; dim=esc"[2m"; dimgray=esc"[2;37m"
        if (used_pct<=40) pct_color=bold esc"[97m"
        else if (used_pct<=60) pct_color=bold esc"[33m"
        else if (used_pct<=75) pct_color=bold esc"[38;5;208m"
        else pct_color=bold esc"[91m"
        bar=dim"["reset
        for(i=0;i<base_chars;i++)   bar=bar yellow "\342\226\223" reset
        for(i=0;i<conv_chars;i++)   bar=bar cyan   "\342\226\223" reset
        for(i=0;i<unused_chars;i++) bar=bar gray   "\342\226\221" reset
        bar=bar dim"]"reset
        if (ctx_size>=1000000) size_str=sprintf("%.0fM",ctx_size/1000000)
        else if (ctx_size>=1000) size_str=sprintf("%.0fk",ctx_size/1000)
        else size_str=sprintf("%d",ctx_size)
        pct_int=int(used_pct+0.5)
        printf "%s " pct_color pct_int "%" reset " " dimgray "of " size_str reset, bar
    }')
else
    ctx_display=$(printf '\033[2mctx:n/a\033[0m')
fi

fmt_tokens() {
    echo "$1" | awk '{
        if ($1>=1000000) printf "%.1fM",$1/1000000
        else if ($1>=1000) printf "%.1fk",$1/1000
        else printf "%d",$1
    }'
}
in_fmt=$(fmt_tokens "$total_in")
out_fmt=$(fmt_tokens "$total_out")

cost=$(echo "$total_in $total_out $cache_write $cache_read" | awk '{
    cost=($1/1000000*3.00)+($2/1000000*15.00)+($3/1000000*3.75)+($4/1000000*0.30)
    if(cost<0.01) printf "$%.4f",cost
    else if(cost<1.00) printf "$%.3f",cost
    else printf "$%.2f",cost
}')

elapsed="--:--"
if [ -n "$transcript" ] && [ -f "$transcript" ]; then
    birth=$(stat --format="%W" "$transcript" 2>/dev/null)
    if [ -z "$birth" ] || [ "$birth" = "0" ]; then
        birth=$(stat --format="%Y" "$transcript" 2>/dev/null)
    fi
    if [ -n "$birth" ] && [ "$birth" != "0" ]; then
        now=$(date +%s); diff=$((now-birth))
        h=$((diff/3600)); m=$(((diff%3600)/60)); s=$((diff%60))
        if [ "$h" -gt 0 ]; then elapsed=$(printf "%dh%02dm" "$h" "$m")
        else elapsed=$(printf "%dm%02ds" "$m" "$s"); fi
    fi
fi

dim=$'\033[2m'; bold=$'\033[1m'; cyan=$'\033[36m'; reset=$'\033[0m'
gray=$'\033[90m'; proj_color=$'\033[1;38;5;220m'
sess_label=$'\033[1;97m'; sess_val=$'\033[37m'
tok_in_label=$'\033[34m'; tok_in_val=$'\033[94m'
tok_out_label=$'\033[35m'; tok_out_val=$'\033[95m'
cost_label=$'\033[32m'; cost_val=$'\033[92m'
dimgray=$'\033[2;37m'

if [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_NAME:-}" ]; then proj_name="$CLAUDE_PROJECT_NAME"
elif [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then proj_name=$(basename "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR")
else proj_name=$(basename "$PWD"); fi

ver_color=$'\033[2;37m'
cli_version="${CLAUDE_VERSION:-}"
if [ -z "$cli_version" ]; then
    cli_version=$(claude --version 2>/dev/null | grep -oE '[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' | head -1)
fi

agent_bold=$'\033[1;97m'; agent_plain=$'\033[37m'
if [ -n "${CLAUDE_AGENT_NAME:-}" ]; then
    agent_str="${agent_bold}${CLAUDE_AGENT_NAME}${reset}"
elif [ -n "${CLAUDE_SUBAGENT_TYPE:-}" ]; then
    agent_str="${agent_bold}${CLAUDE_SUBAGENT_TYPE}${reset}"
else
    agent_str="${agent_plain}Claude Code${reset}"
fi

printf '%s[%s%s%s%s]%s' "$dim" "$reset" "$proj_color" "$proj_name" "$reset$dim" "$reset"

if [ -n "$cli_version" ]; then
    printf ' %s[CC v%s]%s' "$ver_color" "$cli_version" "$reset"
fi

printf ' %s[%s%s%s%s%s]%s' "$dim" "$reset" "$bold" "$cyan" "$model" "$reset$dim" "$reset"

if [ -n "$claude_md_lines" ]; then
    printf ' %s-%s %smd:%s%s%s%s' "$gray" "$reset" "$gray" "$reset" "$sess_val" "$claude_md_lines" "$reset"
fi

printf ' %b' "$ctx_display"

printf ' %s-%s %sin:%s%s%s' "$gray" "$reset" "$tok_in_label" "$reset" "$tok_in_val" "$in_fmt"
printf ' %sout:%s%s%s' "$tok_out_label" "$reset" "$tok_out_val" "$out_fmt"
printf ' %scost:%s%s%s%s' "$cost_label" "$reset" "$cost_val" "$cost" "$reset"
printf ' %ssession:%s%s%s%s' "$sess_label" "$reset" "$sess_val" "$elapsed" "$reset"

printf '      %s>%s %s %s<%s' "$dimgray" "$reset" "$agent_str" "$dimgray" "$reset"

printf "\n"

        for(i=0;i<unused_chars;i++) bar=bar gray   "\342\226\221" reset
        bar=bar dim"]"reset
        if (ctx_size>=1000000) size_str=sprintf("%.0fM",ctx_size/1000000)
        else if (ctx_size>=1000) size_str=sprintf("%.0fk",ctx_size/1000)
        else size_str=sprintf("%d",ctx_size)
        pct_int=int(used_pct+0.5)
        printf "%s " pct_color pct_int "%" reset " " dimgray "of " size_str reset, bar
    }')
else
    ctx_display=$(printf '\033[2mctx:n/a\033[0m')
fi

fmt_tokens() {
    echo "$1" | awk '{
        if ($1>=1000000) printf "%.1fM",$1/1000000
        else if ($1>=1000) printf "%.1fk",$1/1000
        else printf "%d",$1
    }'
}
in_fmt=$(fmt_tokens "$total_in")
out_fmt=$(fmt_tokens "$total_out")

cost=$(echo "$total_in $total_out $cache_write $cache_read" | awk '{
    cost=($1/1000000*3.00)+($2/1000000*15.00)+($3/1000000*3.75)+($4/1000000*0.30)
    if(cost<0.01) printf "$%.4f",cost
    else if(cost<1.00) printf "$%.3f",cost
    else printf "$%.2f",cost
}')

elapsed="--:--"
if [ -n "$transcript" ] && [ -f "$transcript" ]; then
    birth=$(stat --format="%W" "$transcript" 2>/dev/null)
    if [ -z "$birth" ] || [ "$birth" = "0" ]; then
        birth=$(stat --format="%Y" "$transcript" 2>/dev/null)
    fi
    if [ -n "$birth" ] && [ "$birth" != "0" ]; then
        now=$(date +%s); diff=$((now-birth))
        h=$((diff/3600)); m=$(((diff%3600)/60)); s=$((diff%60))
        if [ "$h" -gt 0 ]; then elapsed=$(printf "%dh%02dm" "$h" "$m")
        else elapsed=$(printf "%dm%02ds" "$m" "$s"); fi
    fi
fi

dim=$'\033[2m'; bold=$'\033[1m'; cyan=$'\033[36m'; reset=$'\033[0m'
gray=$'\033[90m'; proj_color=$'\033[1;38;5;220m'
sess_label=$'\033[1;97m'; sess_val=$'\033[37m'
tok_in_label=$'\033[34m'; tok_in_val=$'\033[94m'
tok_out_label=$'\033[35m'; tok_out_val=$'\033[95m'
cost_label=$'\033[32m'; cost_val=$'\033[92m'
dimgray=$'\033[2;37m'

if [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_NAME:-}" ]; then proj_name="$CLAUDE_PROJECT_NAME"
elif [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then proj_name=$(basename "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR")
else proj_name=$(basename "$PWD"); fi

ver_color=$'\033[2;37m'
cli_version="${CLAUDE_VERSION:-}"
if [ -z "$cli_version" ]; then
    cli_version=$(claude --version 2>/dev/null | grep -oE '[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' | head -1)
fi

agent_bold=$'\033[1;97m'; agent_plain=$'\033[37m'
if [ -n "${CLAUDE_AGENT_NAME:-}" ]; then
    agent_str="${agent_bold}${CLAUDE_AGENT_NAME}${reset}"
elif [ -n "${CLAUDE_SUBAGENT_TYPE:-}" ]; then
    agent_str="${agent_bold}${CLAUDE_SUBAGENT_TYPE}${reset}"
else
    agent_str="${agent_plain}Claude Code${reset}"
fi

printf '%s[%s%s%s%s]%s' "$dim" "$reset" "$proj_color" "$proj_name" "$reset$dim" "$reset"

if [ -n "$cli_version" ]; then
    printf ' %s[CC v%s]%s' "$ver_color" "$cli_version" "$reset"
fi

printf ' %s[%s%s%s%s%s]%s' "$dim" "$reset" "$bold" "$cyan" "$model" "$reset$dim" "$reset"

if [ -n "$claude_md_lines" ]; then
    printf ' %s-%s %smd:%s%s%s%s' "$gray" "$reset" "$gray" "$reset" "$sess_val" "$claude_md_lines" "$reset"
fi

printf ' %b' "$ctx_display"

printf ' %s-%s %sin:%s%s%s' "$gray" "$reset" "$tok_in_label" "$reset" "$tok_in_val" "$in_fmt"
printf ' %sout:%s%s%s' "$tok_out_label" "$reset" "$tok_out_val" "$out_fmt"
printf ' %scost:%s%s%s%s' "$cost_label" "$reset" "$cost_val" "$cost" "$reset"
printf ' %ssession:%s%s%s%s' "$sess_label" "$reset" "$sess_val" "$elapsed" "$reset"

printf '      %s>%s %s %s<%s' "$dimgray" "$reset" "$agent_str" "$dimgray" "$reset"

printf "\n"

lastPrompt: I tried it on a different claude code instance on the same version of linux and it's not doing it so it's something here.  Odd.

aiTitle: Debug SSH paste functionality in Claude Code

content.text:
The statusline script is fine — just formatting, no terminal mode changes.

One thing stands out: `TERM=xterm` (not `xterm-256color`). Check what the other instance has:

```sh
echo $TERM
```

Some TUI apps conditionally enable mouse reporting based on terminal capabilities advertised by `TERM`. If the other machine reports `xterm-256color` or `screen-256color`, Claude Code might be initializing mouse mode differently here.

Also worth checking — what plugins does the other instance have enabled? This one has 5 plugins including playwright, which is unusual. If the other is a clean install with no plugins, that's a meaningful difference.

You could test by temporarily disabling all plugins in `~/.claude/settings.json` — just remove the `enabledPlugins` block — restart Claude Code, and see if the behavior changes.

message.content: try disabling the plugins and see if it fixes it

content.text: Done. You'll need to restart Claude Code for the plugin change to take effect. Once you're back in, test paste and mouse selection and let me know if it's fixed.
